reviewer.name: OpenAI Codex
reviewer.responsibility: Claim extraction, primary-source mapping, scope correction, and verification design

claims.statement: PHP variables use a dollar sign followed by a case-sensitive variable name.
Assignment is by value by default, while reference assignment creates aliasing.
Scope and initialization must be checked at the use site.

verifications.method: Assign a known value and confirm the next read or output uses that value.
verifications.expectedResult: The next read or output uses that value.

verifications.method: Change the value once and identify which later expression observes the change.
verifications.expectedResult: The check exposes whether php variable behaves as documented: Change the value once and identify which later expression observes the change.

verifications.method: Check scope and type at the use site instead of assuming the variable is available or unchanged.
verifications.expectedResult: The check exposes whether php variable behaves as documented: Check scope and type at the use site instead of assuming the variable is available or unchanged.

limitations: PHP variable names are case-sensitive and have defined naming rules.
Assignment is by value by default, while references have different aliasing behavior.
Availability depends on scope, and reading an undefined variable produces a warning in current PHP.
Independent human review remains required before promotion to gold.
